label_vocabulary.@deep-work: Task requires sustained focus and uninterrupted concentration. Schedule only in peak energy windows (morning or afternoon primary peak). Never schedule back-to-back without a real break. Can schedule at late night after dinner if no other options, but not after 1:30am.
label_vocabulary.@admin: Low-cognitive overhead task. Emails, updates, coordination, filing.
label_vocabulary.@waiting: Blocked on someone else. Never schedule automatically. Only surface in weekly review.
label_vocabulary.@quick: Under 15 minutes. Batch into transition gaps between larger blocks.
label_vocabulary.@in-progress: Partially completed. Has remaining duration already updated. Treat as higher urgency than unstarted tasks of same priority.
label_vocabulary.@recurring-review: Weekly review task. Do not schedule into daily plan unless explicitly requested.

hard: Never schedule anything during the 90-minute morning buffer after wake
Meetings, calls, and events are immovable — task blocks always move around them
Always add 15-minute buffer before and after Flamingo (meeting/call) events
Always add 30-minute buffer before and after Banana (event) events
Minimum 5-minute gap between any two task blocks
On weekends (Sat/Sun), nothing before 1pm regardless of calendar
Tasks labeled @waiting are never scheduled automatically
Tasks labeled @deep-work must only be placed in peak energy windows (morning through early afternoon) or late night after dinner, never back-to-back without a real break, and not after 1:30am
soft: P1 tasks get the first available deep work block
If a task has been pushed 3+ times, flag it as at-risk
Tasks labeled @quick are batched into transition gaps, not given their own blocks
Tasks labeled @in-progress are treated as higher urgency than unstarted tasks of the same priority level
Deep work tasks (cognitive load: high) should be scheduled in the first half of the working day when possible
Sequence tasks within a block from smaller to larger to build momentum (Cal Newport momentum principle)
